record_id_from_source_name.description: Record identifier derived from the report/source filename.

record_id.description: Unique REDCap record identifier found in the report, if any.

report_date.description: Date the test/report was performed. Prefer YYYY-MM-DD when possible.

not_report.description: Set to not_report if this is not a pathology report, flow_citometry if it is a flow cytometry report, otherwise null.

text_from_report.description: All microscopy and immunohistochemistry text from the report, output in English. The report may be in any language; translate it into English.

extraction_confidence.description: Overall confidence that the coded marker results are correct and complete: 'high' = text clear and results explicit; 'medium' = some ambiguity; 'low' = text garbled/OCR-poor or results unclear. Judge legibility and result clarity, not the diagnosis.

extraction_confidence_reason.description: One short sentence on what made the markers easy or hard to determine (e.g. clear text, OCR garbling, ambiguous or missing results).

additional_markers.description: List EVERY immunohistochemistry or flow-cytometry marker reported in the document. One entry per marker. Use null or an empty array if no markers are reported.

marker.description: Standardized marker/antigen name in English (the antigen the antibody detects), e.g. CD20, CD3, Ki-67, Cyclin D1, TdT, c-Myc, BCL2, MUM1. Do NOT use the vendor, catalog code, or antibody clone as the name (e.g. DAKO, polyclonal, clone L26 / Ber-H2 / MIB-1 / EP12 / EP266). If only a clone/vendor code is given and the antigen is NOT named, do not guess a CD/antigen number — a clone code is not a CD number (clone 124 is not CD124); keep the verbatim clone text as the marker name. Only convert textbook-certain clones (L26 -> CD20, MIB-1 -> Ki-67, Ber-H2 -> CD30), and prefer antigen names stated in the microscopy narrative. Translate non-English names to standard nomenclature (e.g. Кі-67 -> Ki-67, Циклін D1 -> Cyclin D1). Put the original written text (vendor / clone / percentage) in raw_text.

value.description: 1 is positive, 0 is negative, 2 is not done. Interpret the equivalent terms in whatever language the report uses.

raw_text.description: The original result text for this marker as written in the report, if any (e.g. 'positive in 30% of cells').
